record.field: Tools to read, list, and query Odoo models and records
Tools to create, update, delete, and execute methods on database records
Tools to inspect and search custom module and addons code
High-risk tools including safe_eval scripting, EChart visualizations, module compilers, and subagent queries

Discover installed Odoo models in the database. Use when the user asks: "what models exist", "which Odoo models are available", "show me all models", or before using search_read on an unfamiliar model. Supports regex filter on technical names.

Retrieve the FIELD definitions of an Odoo model (field names, types, relations). Call this ONCE per model when you are not 100% certain of field names. After calling it, immediately proceed to search_read or execute_orm — do NOT call get_model_schema again on the same model. IMPORTANT: This tool shows FIELDS only, NOT Python methods. If you need to find what method to call on a model (e.g. action_confirm, action_quotation_send_email), use search_module_code to find the method name, then call it via execute_orm. Never use get_model_schema to look up methods — it will never show them.

Find, list, filter, or count records in any Odoo model. ALWAYS call this tool when the user asks: "show me all", "find customers", "list invoices", "get orders", "how many records", "search for", "fetch", "retrieve". Never answer data questions from memory — always query with this tool first. IMPORTANT: Never guess field names. If you are not 100% certain which fields exist on the model, call get_model_schema first, then use the exact field names it returns.

Get full details of a single record by its database ID. Use when the user says: "show me record #X", "get details of ID", "open record", or after search_read returns an ID and the user wants full field details.

Aggregate and group database records for statistics and reports. Use when the user asks: "total sales by month", "count orders per customer", "sum revenue by product", "how much in total", "group by", "breakdown of", "average", "statistics". Never calculate aggregates manually from raw records — use this tool. Call directly without schema lookup — you know the field names for standard Odoo models.

Create a new record in Odoo. Use when the user says: "create a", "add a new", "make a new", "register a", "insert a". Examples: create a customer, create a sale order, add a new product. You know the required fields for common models — call this directly without schema lookup.

Update fields on one or more existing records. Use when the user says: "update", "change", "set the", "modify", "edit record", "rename". Always use search_read first to get the record IDs before updating.
Permanently delete records from Odoo. Use when the user says: "delete", "remove", "unlink", "erase". Always confirm record IDs with search_read before deleting. This action is irreversible.

Run an Odoo workflow action or business method on records. Use when the user says: "confirm the order", "validate the invoice", "approve", "cancel", "post", "mark as done", "send", "reset to draft". Trigger: any workflow state change that isn't just a field write.

Search the module source code for classes, methods, or patterns. Use when the user asks: "where is X defined", "find the class", "search codebase for", "which file contains", "show me the implementation of". Returns file path, line number, and matching content.

Read the contents of a source file from the module directory. Use when the user says: "show me the file", "read the code in", "open the file", "what is in", or after code_search returns a file path and you need to see the full content.

Execute a Python script in Odoo's safe_eval sandbox with full ORM access. Use for complex multi-step operations that no single tool covers: "run this script", "compute and update", "bulk operation", "custom logic". Has access to env, user, Command, json, datetime, requests.

Create and save an Apache ECharts visualization. Use when user asks: "create a chart", "visualize", "plot", "show me a graph". SINGLE tool call — do NOT call read_group separately first. Write data_code Python using ECharts dataset format: query ORM inside data_code, build source as list-of-lists (first row = headers), return complete options dict. BAR/LINE: result=env['sale.order'].read_group(domain=[['date_order','>=','2026-06-01'],['state','not in',['cancel']]],fields=['amount_total:sum'],groupby=['date_order:day']); source=[['Date','Sales']]+[[str(r.get('date_order:day','')),r.get('amount_total',0)] for r in result]; return {'title':{'text':'June Sales'},'dataset':{'source':source},'xAxis':{'type':'category'},'yAxis':{'type':'value'},'series':[{'type':'bar','name':'Sales'}]}. PIE: result=env['sale.order'].read_group(domain=[],fields=['amount_total:sum'],groupby=['partner_id']); source=[['Customer','Amount']]+[[r['partner_id'][1],r.get('amount_total',0)] for r in result if r.get('partner_id')]; return {'title':{'text':'By Customer'},'dataset':{'source':source},'series':[{'type':'pie','radius':'60%','encode':{'itemName':'Customer','value':'Amount'}}]}. RULES: always use dataset.source, never xAxis.data or series.data, data_code must end with return dict.

Delegate a task to a different AI agent configured in the system. Use when the user says: "ask the other agent", "use the specialist agent", "delegate to", or when the current agent lacks tools the target agent has. Max recursion depth: 3.
